database.title: Sesi Built-in Functions Reference
database.description: A comprehensive guide to the core built-in functions, standard library modules, and system operations available in the Sesi programming language.
database.summary: Sesi provides a robust set of built-in functions for I/O, type conversion, collection manipulation, file system interaction, networking, and system-level tasks. It supports structured error handling, module imports via a hierarchical resolution path, and includes specialized functions for workflow orchestration and tool definition.

code_examples.code:
define_tool("summarizer", fn(t) { return "Summary: " + t }, "Summarizes text")
let result = workflow([{"prompt": "Summarize:"}], "Input text")

import { PI, sqrt } from "std/math"
import { sleep } from "std/time"

key_rules: Paths for file system functions are resolved relative to the current working directory.
The `workflow` function automatically chains step outputs as input for subsequent steps.
Module resolution follows a strict priority: Script directory > Current working directory > SESI_PATH > ~/.sesi/lib.
Structured error handling is performed via `try/catch` blocks; `raise_error` terminates execution.
Collection functions like `push` modify arrays in-place.
Standard library modules (v1.3+) must be imported using the `import { ... } from "std/..."` syntax.
Functions like `num`, `len`, `keys`, and `values` return `null` upon failure or invalid input.

database.title: Data Analyzer and CSV Processor
database.description: A comprehensive utility module for parsing, manipulating, and performing statistical analysis on CSV-formatted data.
database.summary: This module provides a robust suite of tools for processing tabular data, including CSV parsing, record filtering, and sorting. It includes advanced analytical functions for computing descriptive statistics such as mean, median, mode, and standard deviation. Additionally, the library supports data validation and serialization into multiple formats, including JSON, Excel-compatible XML, and Word-compatible XML. The implementation relies on custom algorithms for tasks like square root calculation and sorting to maintain independence from external dependencies.

code_examples.description: Parsing a CSV string and computing the average of a numeric field
code_examples.code:
let data = parse_csv("name,score\nAlice,85\nBob,92");
let avg = compute_average(data, "score");

code_examples.description: Validating records for required fields and exporting to JSON
code_examples.code:
let issues = validate_records(data, ["name", "score"]);
let jsonOutput = export_to_json(data);

key_rules: Numerical operations require explicit coercion using the '- 0' syntax to ensure proper type handling.
The module uses a custom Newton-Raphson implementation for square root calculations as no built-in function is available.
CSV parsing assumes the first row of the input string contains the headers.
The sorting implementation uses a standard Bubble Sort algorithm, which may have performance implications on very large datasets.

database.title: Sesi DB Helper
database.description: A lightweight, zero-dependency flat-file JSON database utility for persistent stateful storage in Sesi.
database.summary: This module provides a comprehensive suite of CRUD operations for managing local JSON-based data collections. It includes features for record insertion with auto-generated IDs, advanced filtering with AND logic, pagination, and sorting. Additionally, it supports collection management, bulk deletion, and built-in backup/restore functionality to ensure data integrity. The database is stored as a single flat file, making it ideal for simple, local-first applications.

code_examples.description: Initialize the database and insert a new record into a collection.
code_examples.code:
init_db("data.json");
insert_record("data.json", "users", {"name": "Alice", "role": "admin"});

code_examples.description: Retrieve paginated records from a collection with a filter.
code_examples.code: let results = find_records_paginated("data.json", "users", "role", "admin", 10, 0);

key_rules: The database must be initialized via init_db or read_db before performing operations to ensure the file structure exists.
Records are automatically assigned a unique 'id' field upon insertion using a combination of time and random values.
All write operations (insert, update, delete) trigger a full file rewrite, which may impact performance on very large datasets.
The module relies on standard JSON parsing and file system access, requiring appropriate read/write permissions for the target path.

database.title: Sesi Programming Language
database.description: A highly legible, buildable programming language designed for parsing text, orchestrating shell commands, and interacting with the file system. It features a tree-walking interpreter, static type safety with runtime flexibility, and native integration for external logic execution.
database.summary: Sesi is a TypeScript-based language that prioritizes simplicity and transparency. It provides a clean syntax for standard programming constructs (variables, functions, control flow) alongside specialized primitives for external service interaction, structured output, and memory management. The implementation is modular, consisting of a recursive descent parser and a tree-walking interpreter, supported by a comprehensive suite of built-in functions for I/O, networking, and concurrency.

code_examples.code: fn add(a: number, b: number) -> number {return a + b}
if condition { print "True" } else { print "False" }

key_rules: The language uses a tree-walking interpreter for clarity and maintainability.
Expressions ending in '}' do not require explicit statement termination (semicolons or newlines).

database.title: Programmatic Markdown-to-HTML Compiler
database.description: A high-performance markdown parser and layout engine that uses string segmentation instead of regex to convert markdown files into styled HTML.
database.summary: This module provides a comprehensive suite of tools for parsing markdown syntax, including inline formatting like bold, italics, links, and code spans, as well as block-level elements such as tables, blockquotes, task lists, and footnotes. It features a modular design with both original and extended parsing functions, allowing for flexible compilation of individual files or entire directories. The engine includes built-in styling for generated HTML elements and supports advanced features like Mermaid diagram blocks and footnote collection. It is designed for efficiency, avoiding heavy regex engines in favor of custom string-splitting logic.

code_examples.description: Compile a single markdown file using the full-featured parser.
code_examples.code: compile_markdown_file_full("input.md", "output.html", "My Document")

code_examples.description: Batch compile all markdown files within a specific directory.
code_examples.code: compile_markdown_dir("./docs", "./dist")

key_rules: The parser uses custom string segmentation logic rather than standard regular expressions for performance.
Inline formatting like bold and italics must be balanced within the same line to be parsed correctly.
Table parsing requires a header row followed by a separator row of dashes to trigger the table compilation logic.
Footnote definitions must follow the '[^key]: text' format to be correctly extracted by the collector.
The compiler automatically handles block-level state transitions (e.g., closing open lists or tables) when encountering new block types or code markers.

database.title: webpage-factory Project Documentation
database.description: A self-contained Sesi project designed for programmatically compiling responsive HTML pages, data reports, and visual charts.
database.summary: The webpage-factory project provides a comprehensive suite of Sesi modules for building static web content without client-side JavaScript. It includes tools for site construction, markdown parsing, SVG-based data visualization, and CSV data analysis. Additionally, it features a flat-file JSON database engine for persistent storage and management. The project is managed via a CLI that supports compilation, linting, and file encryption tasks.

code_examples.code: npm start

code_examples.description: Import and use a site-builder function
code_examples.code:
import { generate_page } from "site-builder"; generate_page("My Title", "

Hello World

");

key_rules: All visual charts are rendered as pure HTML/CSS/SVG with zero client-side JavaScript.
The project utilizes a custom Sesi interpreter and CLI for all compilation and execution tasks.
Database operations are performed using a flat-file JSON storage engine.
The site-builder module provides functions for generating responsive layouts such as bento grids, accordions, and modals.

database.title: Sesi Native Webpage Generator
database.description: A comprehensive library for programmatically generating static websites, UI components, and layout assets using Sesi.
database.summary: This module provides a suite of functions for building modular web pages, including layout factories, interactive UI components like accordions and modals, and SEO metadata generation. It features robust error handling and input validation wrappers to ensure reliable site construction. The library also includes advanced capabilities such as concurrent page building using worker scripts and AI-assisted content generation. It is designed to streamline the creation of structured, responsive, and accessible web interfaces.

code_examples.description: Generating a simple webpage with a hero banner and SEO metadata.

code_examples.code:
let css = generate_responsive_layout('.container', 3, 1);
write_file('layout.css', css);

key_rules: All page generation functions rely on the base 'create_header' and 'create_footer' templates for structural consistency.
The library uses 'assert_' prefixed functions to enforce strict input validation for strings, arrays, and numbers.
Concurrent builds require the use of 'concurrent_build', which spawns separate worker processes to handle asset generation in parallel.
AI-assisted functions like 'generatePageContent' include fallback mechanisms to ensure static content generation if the model call fails.

database.title: Sesi Language Specification (v1.5)
database.description: A concise, legible, and practical programming language designed for frictionless script development, process orchestration, and integration with external models.
database.summary: Sesi is a minimal, buildable language featuring a clean syntax and a robust module system. It emphasizes transparency and practicality with built-in support for process spawning, file system operations, and cross-platform library management.

code_examples.code:
let x = 10
let y = 20
print x + y
fn add(a: number, b: number) {print a + b}

import {add} from "math"
let result = add(10, 20)

key_rules: Execution is strictly blocking; concurrency is handled via spawn().
Variables are declared using 'let'; functions use 'fn'.
Module resolution follows a strict priority: Script directory -> Working directory -> SESI_PATH -> ~/.sesi/lib.
Structured output requires a schema definition and provides type-safe access to returned fields.
Null propagation: Operations on null return null without throwing exceptions.
Lexical scoping applies; inner scopes shadow outer scopes.
Requires Node.js 18+.

database.title: Sesi Native Brutalist Visual Charting Engine
database.description: A specialized library for generating high-density, static dashboard visualizations using pure HTML, CSS, and SVG without client-side JavaScript.
database.summary: This engine provides a suite of functions to render various data visualizations including bar charts, sparklines, gauges, pie/donut charts, heatmaps, scatter plots, radar charts, funnels, candlesticks, and treemaps. It utilizes a 'brutalist' aesthetic, characterized by high-contrast colors and clean, geometric SVG paths. The library is designed for low-latency, server-side generation of static dashboard components. It includes internal trigonometric helpers for SVG arc calculations and dynamic color-coding logic based on data thresholds.

code_examples.description: Generating a simple horizontal bar chart
code_examples.code:
let data = [{"label": "CPU", "value": 45}, {"label": "RAM", "value": 72}];
let chart = compile_bar_chart(data, "System Load");

code_examples.description: Generating a sparkline from a numeric array
code_examples.code:
let values = [10, 25, 15, 40, 30];
let spark = compile_sparkline(values, "Traffic Trends");

key_rules: All charts are rendered as static HTML/SVG strings; no client-side JavaScript is required for interactivity or rendering.
The engine uses a specific 'brutalist' color palette, primarily relying on neon greens, reds, and yellows for status indicators.
SVG coordinate systems are hard-coded for specific viewboxes, requiring data normalization within the functions.
The library includes custom trigonometric approximations (Taylor series) for sine and cosine to handle SVG path geometry.
